subject: Andrej Karpathy
evidence_standard: L1 = verbatim traceable | L2 = reported, reliable secondary | L3 = inferred from pattern

biography.raised: Moved to Toronto, Canada at age 15

education.focus: intersection of natural language processing and computer vision

career_timeline.detail: One of the founding members. Contributed to early deep learning research during OpenAI's formative period.

Created and taught 'Convolutional Neural Networks for Visual Recognition' — grew from 150 to 750 students by 2017. Became one of the defining educational resources for deep learning globally. Available for free online.

Led computer vision team for Autopilot, reporting to Elon Musk. Managed data labeling, neural network training, and deployment on custom inference chips. Oversaw Full Self-Driving development. Introduced the 'Software 2.0' framing internally. Left July 2022 after a sabbatical.

Returned February 2023. Built team focused on midtraining and synthetic data generation. Gave 'State of GPT' talk at Microsoft Build 2023 — became one of the most-viewed technical talks on LLMs. Left February 2024.
Founded July 2024. AI education platform — 'AI-native learning.' Builds AI teaching assistants (LLM-backed TAs). Vision: scaling personalized education globally.

key_contributions.contribution: CS231n — Convolutional Neural Networks for Visual Recognition
key_contributions.impact: Became the canonical deep learning curriculum. Made modern deep learning accessible to a generation of practitioners. Still freely available online. More than any single paper, CS231n trained the people who trained today's AI systems.

Articulated the paradigm shift from writing code explicitly (Software 1.0) to specifying datasets and architectures (Software 2.0). Changed how practitioners think about what they're building and what skills matter.
Led the team that built and deployed computer vision at scale on millions of vehicles. Real-world deployment at unprecedented scale — millions of GPT-like inference calls per second.
Free educational series building neural networks from scratch in code. Millions of views. Karpathy-style explanation — deep mathematical grounding + practical implementation simultaneously.
Reference implementations that let anyone understand how RNNs, autograd, and transformers work. nanoGPT especially — a minimal, readable GPT implementation that became the standard teaching tool.

key_decisions.reasoning: Research impact via systems that get deployed > research impact via papers. Chose scale of real-world deployment over academic prestige.
key_decisions.what_it_reveals: Practical builder orientation. Values empirical validation over theoretical elegance.

key_decisions.decision: Moved all CS231n materials public and free
key_decisions.reasoning: Education should scale. The knowledge that builds great AI practitioners shouldn't be locked behind Stanford tuition.
key_decisions.what_it_reveals: Open-source educational ethos. Sees scale of impact through education as valuable as research output.

key_decisions.decision: Built Eureka Labs for AI education (2024)
key_decisions.reasoning: Saw gap: LLMs could function as tireless AI teaching assistants at 1:1 teacher-student ratio globally. Education is one of the highest-leverage applications of AI.
key_decisions.outcome: Early-stage — building LLM-backed teaching assistants for technical subjects.
key_decisions.what_it_reveals: Sees education as the highest leverage application of AI in the near term.

key_decisions.decision: Rejoined OpenAI then left for Anthropic
key_decisions.reasoning: Selective institutional engagement — joins when his marginal contribution is highest. Left OpenAI after one year of return; joined Anthropic for pretraining work.
key_decisions.what_it_reveals: Not institutionally loyal — calibrates where his specific skills are most needed.

documented_positions.on_neural_networks: Software 2.0 — datasets are the new source code. Training a neural network is compiling a dataset into executable behavior. Most AI development is data curation, not algorithm design.
documented_positions.on_scaling: 33 years of AI history shows the fundamental approach (backprop, SGD, neural nets) hasn't changed — scale has. 10M× more compute/data = roughly where we'll be in 33 more years. Scale is the dominant variable.
documented_positions.on_debugging: Neural net training fails silently. Patience and attention to detail are the most important qualities. Start simple — try a BB gun before the Bazooka.
documented_positions.on_LLMs: LLMs are like operating systems for AI — they're the platform, not the app. The hottest new programming language is English. Vibe coding (describing intent, iterating on LLM output) is a real and valid paradigm.
documented_positions.on_education: Every person should have access to a 1:1 AI tutor as good as the best human teacher. Education is the highest-leverage AI application near-term.
documented_positions.on_end_to_end: Trust the gradient. End-to-end learning consistently outperforms hand-engineered pipelines. Don't engineer features — engineer the learning process.
